questionText: A three-digit number is written with the same digit $a$ in every position (for example $444$). Prove that this number is divisible by $3$.

steps.description: Write the number using place value.
steps.explanation: Each digit contributes its face value times its place value; all three digits are $a$.
The number $111$ factors as $3\times37$, which reveals a factor of $3$.
Substituting the factorisation displays a factor of $3$ explicitly.

steps.description: Identify the bracket as an integer.
steps.explanation: Since $a$ is a digit, $37a$ is an integer, so the number is $3\times(\text{integer})$.
A number equal to $3$ times an integer is a multiple of $3$.
A three-digit number with all equal digits equals $111a=3(37a)$, so it is divisible by $3$.

workedSolution.finalAnswer: $\overline{aaa}=100a+10a+a=111a=3(37a)$, a multiple of $3$. Hence it is divisible by $3$. $\blacksquare$
